content: In terminal commands, do not use \n or literal newlines in the commit message as the command will fail.
To write multi-line git commit messages in terminal commands, use multiple `-m` flags for each line of the message.
Use the first `-m` flag for the commit summary and keep it under 50 characters.
Use additional `-m` flags for each detail line in the commit body.
For lists in commit messages, use separate `-m` flags for each list item and start each item with a hyphen for consistency.
Avoid common patterns that cause errors, such as literal newlines, escaped newlines (\n), and backtick or quote wrapping of multi-line strings in commit commands.
